rulings.Case Title: ADAM O'CONNOR VS. SCOTT NEWLIN JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Monetary Santions, And For An Order For Deemed Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, February 24, 2025, line 6, DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE AMBULANCE SERVICE, INC.") Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, For Monetary Santions, And For An Order For Deemed Admissions Granted. No opposition filed, although plaintiff opposed an ex parte application to hear the motion on shortened time. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Further Responses To Defendant'S Supplemental Special Interrogatory Responses (Set 1), Specifically Interrogatories 2 And 4, Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Or, In The Alternative To Compelling Further Responses, (2) Request For Evidentiary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,600.00 Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300; Declaration Of Daniel B. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, February 24, 2025, line 14, 2 - PLAINTIFF CARRIE MYLES' Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Further Responses To Defendant'S Supplemental Special Interrogatory Responses (Set 1), Specifically Interrogatories 2 And 4, Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Or, In The Alternative To Compelling Further Responses, (2) Request For Evidentiary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,600.00 Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Plaintiff Carris Myles' motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 2 and 4 and for evidentiary and monetary sanctions is denied as moot as to interrogatory 2, granted as to interrogatory 4, denied as to evidentiary sanctions, and granted as to monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,000 against defendant Urban Alchemy. Defendant must provide verified further responses by March 7, 2025 and pay the monetary sanctions no later than March 17, 2025. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: YASMIN ABDULMALIK VS. CRUISE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Operative Complaint (C.C.P 473, 576); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Genessis Guevara; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, February 24, 2025, line 9, PLAINTIFF YASMIN ABDULMALIK'S Motion To Amend Operative Complaint (C.C.P 473, 576) Plaintiff's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is denied as to any allegations supporting claims for sex and pregnancy based harassment and granted in all other respects. Plaintiff may file her proposed first amended complaint either deleting her claims for sex and pregnancy based harassment or stating that those claims have been eliminated from the case as a result of the order granting Cruise's motion for judgment on the pleading as to those claims. None of the defendants appear to have any substantive objections to plaintiff alleging additional matters as to her claims other than sex and pregnancy based harassment and California's strong policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings favors allowing her to do so. Plaintiff must file her first amended complaint by March 5, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RACHEL CROCKETT VS. LLOYD & MOUSILLI, PLLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, February 24, 2025, line 13, 2 - PLAINTIFF RACHEL CROCKETT, DANA KEMPLER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. Defendants' "limited" opposition fails to identify any grounds to deny this motion in light of California's strong policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. Defendants mistakenly assert that the moving papers did not include an attorney declaration. Defendants' claim of prejudice is not persuasive given that no trial date has been set. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Conditionally Seal Complaint, Motion For Preliminary Injunction And Supporting Documents; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Monday, February 24, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFF LYDRASOFT, INC.'S Motion To Conditionally Seal Complaint, Motion For Preliminary Injunction And Supporting Documents Plaintiff Lydrasoft, Inc.'s motion to seal documents is denied without prejudice to being re-filed. Lydrasoft seeks to shift the burden to defendant Atos Limited to identify what, if any, documents should be sealed, yet Lydrasoft has not shown that it has personal jurisdiction over Atos or that Atos has received sufficient time to respond to this motion or the complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
